name: Polycarbonate Laser Cleaning Dataset
description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for Polycarbonate. Includes thermal, optical, mechanical, and laser interaction properties validated against industry standards.

license.name: Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International
license.description: Free to share and adapt with attribution

measurementTechnique: Laser ablation testing, material characterization, spectroscopy

includedInDataCatalog.name: Z-Beam Material Properties Database
includedInDataCatalog.description: Comprehensive laser cleaning parameters and material properties for industrial applications

powerRange.description: Optimal average power for Polycarbonate surface cleaning without thermal damage

wavelength.description: Near-IR wavelength providing good absorption in contaminants while minimizing Polycarbonate substrate damage

spotSize.description: Beam spot diameter for precise cleaning with sufficient energy density

repetitionRate.description: Optimal repetition rate for efficient cleaning with adequate cooling between pulses

fluenceThreshold.description: Fluence threshold for contaminant removal while preserving Polycarbonate substrate

pulseWidth.description: Nanosecond pulse duration for effective contaminant removal with controlled thermal penetration

scanSpeed.description: Optimal scanning speed for uniform cleaning coverage

passCount.description: Recommended number of passes for complete contaminant removal

overlapRatio.description: Optimal beam overlap for uniform cleaning without excessive thermal buildup

power.description: Typical range for pulsed fiber lasers in polymer cleaning to avoid thermal damage; starting at 50W for light contamination on PC, max limited by melting point (~147°C) and low thermal conductivity. Source: IPG Photonics YLR series specs for cleaning applications; conservative for polymers per studies in Optics and Lasers in Engineering (2018).

scan_speed.description: Slower speeds for polymers to control heat buildup; 500 mm/s optimal for uniform contaminant removal without substrate ablation. Range adjusts for light (faster) vs heavy contamination. Source: Industry standards from Laserax equipment manuals; case studies in Journal of Cleaner Production (2020).

pulse_width.description: Nanosecond regime preferred for PC to balance ablation efficiency and minimal heat-affected zone (HAZ <10μm); picosecond (ps) reduces HAZ further but increases cost. Femtosecond risks over-ablation. Source: Laser physics research in Applied Surface Science (2019) on polymer ablation thresholds.

repetition_rate.description: 30 kHz balances cleaning speed and heat accumulation for PC's low thermal conductivity; higher rates (>50 kHz) accelerate process but risk melting. Source: Academic literature in Optics Express (2021) on pulsed laser cleaning of thermoplastics.

energy_density.description: Fluence below PC damage threshold (~1-2 J/cm² at 1064 nm due to moderate absorption); 0.5 J/cm² for safe contaminant removal. Conservative to prevent charring. Source: Ablation threshold studies in Journal of Applied Physics (2017) for polycarbonate at near-IR wavelengths.

spot_size.description: 100 μm diameter for precise surface preparation on PC; smaller for fine details, larger for broad areas. Matches typical Gaussian beam profiles. Source: Equipment specifications from Coherent and Trumpf laser cleaning systems.

pass_count.description: Multi-pass (2 recommended) for complete removal of contaminants without single-pass overheating; single pass for very light soiling. Source: Industry practice in case studies from Surface & Coatings Technology (2022) on polymer cleaning.

overlap_ratio.description: 60% overlap ensures uniform coverage and avoids gaps in cleaning; higher for heavy contamination to build fluence gradually. Source: Process optimization studies in Lasers in Manufacturing and Materials Processing (2020).
